description: Complete rewrite of the QR check-in system: simplify QR format, rewrite scanner handler, add scan_checkin() to crud.py, add auto_close_events() background task to main.py, update middleware bypass regex, and regenerate zip
steps: Step 1 - Simplify QR data format in ticket_generator.py: Change generate_qr_data() to produce the new format: https://t.me/{BOT_USERNAME}?start=checkin_{ticket_id}_{pin}. This requires ticket_id to be passed in. Update the function signature to accept ticket_id and pin instead of event_id/user_telegram_id/security_hash. The caller in crud.py create_ticket() must be updated accordingly (generate QR data AFTER ticket is committed and has an ID).

Step 2 - Update crud.py create_ticket(): After the ticket is committed and has a primary key ID, call generate_qr_data(ticket.id, pin) to get the new-format QR data, then update the ticket's qr_data field. Remove the security_hash from the QR data generation (keep it in the DB for legacy but it's no longer used in QR).

Step 3 - Add scan_checkin() function in crud.py: New async function scan_checkin(ticket_id: int, pin: str, scanner_tg_id: int) that: (1) finds ticket by ID, (2) verifies PIN matches, (3) checks scanner is admin (SUPER_ADMIN_ID) or president (is_cp=1), (4) checks event is ACTIVE, (5) checks current time is within event_date + 1 hour (or no event_date set), (6) marks registration status as ATTENDED and sets check_in_time, (7) marks ticket as used (is_used=True, used_at=now), (8) returns (True, message, extra_data) on success. Does NOT give points - that happens in auto_close.

Step 4 - Add auto_close_events() function in crud.py: New async function that: (1) finds all ACTIVE events where event_date + 1 hour < now, (2) for each event: gives attendance_points to all ATTENDED registrations (updating users.total_points and user_status), marks event as COMPLETED, (3) returns a list of report dicts containing event info, attendee lists, and point info for notifications.

Step 5 - Completely rewrite scanner.py: The handle_deep_link handler must parse the new format 'checkin_{ticket_id}_{pin}' (args starts with 'checkin_'). Extract ticket_id and pin, call scan_checkin(), send confirmation to scanner, send notification to ticket owner. Keep the /scan command and /mytickets command as-is (they work fine).

Step 6 - Add background task to main.py: After bot initialization, start an asyncio background task that runs auto_close_events() every 5 minutes. On each run, if events were closed, send the attendance report to the event creator (use created_by field to find their telegram_id) and SUPER_ADMIN_ID. The report message includes: event name, total registered count, total attended count, and list of attendees.
Step 7 - Update check_sub.py middleware: Change the bypass regex from r'^/start(@\w+)?\s+chk_' to r'^/start(@\w+)?\s+checkin_' to match the new QR deep link format.
Step 8 - Regenerate UFQ_SYSTEM_v3_fixed.zip: Run zip -r UFQ_SYSTEM_v3_fixed.zip UFQ_SYSTEM_v3/ -x '*__pycache__*' '*.pyc' from /projects/sandbox/ufqbot/
Step 9 - Commit all changes to git with descriptive message
acceptance_criteria: create_ticket() generates QR data with the ticket's actual primary key ID after commit
scan_checkin() exists in crud.py and accepts (ticket_id, pin, scanner_tg_id), returns (success, msg, extra)
scan_checkin() does NOT award points - only marks ATTENDED and ticket as used
auto_close_events() exists in crud.py and processes expired ACTIVE events (event_date + 1 hour < now)
auto_close_events() awards attendance_points to ATTENDED users and marks events COMPLETED
main.py has a background task calling auto_close_events() every 5 minutes
All Python files parse without syntax errors
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/ufqbot/UFQ_SYSTEM_v3/event_bot && python -c "import ast; files=['bot/handlers/scanner.py','bot/database/crud.py','bot/utils/ticket_generator.py','bot/main.py','bot/middlewares/check_sub.py']; [ast.parse(open(f).read()) for f in files]; print('All files parse OK')"

cd /projects/sandbox/ufqbot/UFQ_SYSTEM_v3/event_bot && python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); from bot.utils.ticket_generator import generate_qr_data; result=generate_qr_data(42,'123456'); assert 'checkin_42_123456' in result; print('QR format OK:', result)"
cd /projects/sandbox/ufqbot/UFQ_SYSTEM_v3/event_bot && python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); from bot.database.crud import scan_checkin, auto_close_events; print('Imports OK')"
findings: All steps implemented successfully. generate_qr_data now takes (ticket_id, pin) and produces checkin_{id}_{pin} format. scan_checkin() and auto_close_events() added to crud.py. scanner.py deep link handler parses new format. Background task added to main.py. Middleware regex updated. All files parse and imports resolve correctly. Post-review fixes applied: (1) duplicate report guard in main.py auto_close_task, (2) club-scope check added to scan_checkin, (3) batch DB connections in auto_close_events, (4) dead code verify_and_checkin removed.
